name: Instagram Post Reel from URL
description: Verifies the correct Instagram account is active, downloads a video from a URL into a unique album, then uploads it as a Reel with a caption.

params.username: The Instagram username that should be active
params.videoUrl: Direct URL to the video file to download
params.caption: Caption text for the Reel
params.albumName: Unique album name for this download (e.g. 'reel_20260301_001')

steps.goal: Check which Instagram account is currently logged in by looking at the profile tab or the username displayed at the top. If the active account is '{{username}}', proceed (mark done). If a different account is active, tap the username at the top to open the account switcher, then select '{{username}}'. If '{{username}}' is not listed, mark as failed.

Download a video file to the device into a specific album. Use the download action with url {{videoUrl}} and save it as {{albumName}}/reel_video.mp4. The album format (AlbumName/filename) will create a gallery album named '{{albumName}}' under Pictures. After the download completes, mark done.

Navigate to the Instagram Home feed. Tap the Home icon (house icon) at the bottom-left of the screen if you are not already on the home feed. Once on the home feed, look at the TOP-LEFT corner of the screen — there is a '+' icon next to the Instagram logo. Tap that '+' icon at the top-left to open the content creation screen.
You should now be on the content creation screen with a media gallery and tabs at the bottom (POST, REEL, STORY). Tap the 'REEL' tab at the bottom to switch to Reel mode.

You should now be in Reel mode on the content creation screen with a media gallery visible. At the top of the media gallery there is a button labeled 'Recents'. Tap 'Recents' — this will open a list of album options. In that list, find and tap 'All albums' (or 'Other...' or 'Browse folders'). This reveals all available albums on the device. Scroll through the albums and find the one named '{{albumName}}', then tap it to select it.

You should now see the media from the '{{albumName}}' album. There should be one video — select it by tapping on it. After selecting, you may see a trim/edit screen. Tap 'Next' or the forward arrow to proceed to the next screen.

You should now be on the caption/share screen for the Reel. Find the caption text field and type the following caption: '{{caption}}'. After entering the caption, tap 'Share' or 'Post' to publish the Reel. Wait for the upload to begin, then mark done.
